response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished senior individual contributor with strong open-source credentials and a broad full-stack background, but he falls critically short of several non-negotiable requirements for this Head of Development role. Most disqualifyingly, his German is self-assessed as 'Basic,' far below the C1 minimum required for client-facing advisory work in a German IT consulting firm. Beyond the language gap, the resume presents no evidence of team leadership, people management, or client advisory responsibilities — the role's core competencies — and neither Python nor UML appear anywhere in his skills or project history.
strengths: Genuine open-source track record with multiple published projects and a FOSS4G conference talk, directly aligned with the company's open-source and digital sovereignty focus
Unusually broad technical depth spanning embedded systems, high-throughput services, GIS platforms, cloud infrastructure, and AI tooling across 15+ years at recognized companies
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing culture through Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, and multi-day workshops, suggesting latent mentorship capability
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — the role explicitly requires C1 minimum and centers on client communication and advisory work in a German-market consulting context
Resume reads entirely as an individual contributor; no evidence of team leadership, headcount ownership, performance management, or Head-of-Engineering-level responsibility
Python and UML — both explicitly desired technical skills for this role — are absent from the skills section and do not appear in any project or employment description

key_factors.factor: German language gap (Basic vs. C1 required)
No team leadership or management experience
